successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest with fields id, employeeId, leaveType, startDate, endDate, status, approverEmployeeId, and createdAt.
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports a LeaveRepository interface with create, findById, and findByEmployeeId methods operating on LeaveRequest types imported from leave.model.ts.
src/modules/leave/postgres-leave.repository.ts exists and exports PostgresLeaveRepository implementing LeaveRepository using a pg Pool.
A PostgreSQL migration exists creating the leave_requests table with the specified columns, constraints, and indexes.
Vitest tests verify PostgresLeaveRepository can persist a LeaveRequest, retrieve it by id, and list requests by employeeId, while using the canonical LeaveRequest types.

constraints: Repository pattern: all database access must be abstracted through the LeaveRepository interface.
LeaveRequest types must be defined in src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts and repository code must reference those exported types.
PostgreSQL persistence implementation must conform to the LeaveRepository contract.
No sensitive data may be logged by repository implementations or tests.
All asynchronous repository operations must catch and handle errors; no unhandled promise rejections.
Scope is limited to establishing leave-module data ownership and persistence boundaries before workflow logic.
outOfScope: Leave approval and rejection workflow logic.
Fastify API endpoints for leave management.
Service-layer business logic beyond repository contracts.
Audit-record generation for workflow/state transitions outside repository scaffolding.

ambiguities.description: The intent mentions 'DTOs used by persistence' in leave.model.ts, but the architecture specification only defines LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest.
options: Define only the explicitly specified LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest exports.
Add additional persistence DTO types alongside LeaveRequest.
